claims.text: Large language models struggle to catch errors in their own outputs when the review happens in the same session that produced them.
claims.source_quote: Large language models struggle to catch errors in their own outputs when the review happens in the same session that produced them.

claims.text: Cross-Context Review is a method where the review is conducted in a fresh session with no access to the production conversation history.
claims.source_quote: This paper introduces Cross-Context Review (CCR), a straightforward method where the review is conducted in a fresh session with no access to the production conversation history.
We ran a controlled experiment: 30 artifacts (code, technical documents, presentation scripts)

claims.text: The artifacts included code, technical documents, and presentation scripts.
150 errors were injected into the artifacts.
claims.source_quote: 30 artifacts (code, technical documents, presentation scripts) with 150 injected errors

claims.text: Four review conditions were tested: same-session Self-Review, repeated Self-Review, context-aware Subagent Review, and Cross-Context Review.
claims.source_quote: tested under four review conditions -- same-session Self-Review (SR), repeated Self-Review (SR2), context-aware Subagent Review (SA), and Cross-Context Review (CCR)

claims.text: Cross-Context Review achieved an F1 score of 28.6%.
Same-session Self-Review achieved an F1 score of 24.6%.
Repeated Self-Review achieved an F1 score of 21.7%.
Context-aware Subagent Review achieved an F1 score of 23.8%.
Cross-Context Review outperformed same-session Self-Review with p=0.008 and d=0.52.
Cross-Context Review outperformed repeated Self-Review with p<0.001 and d=0.72.
Cross-Context Review outperformed context-aware Subagent Review with p=0.004 and d=0.57.
Reviewing twice in the same session did not beat reviewing once with p=0.11.
claims.source_quote: reviewing twice in the same session did not beat reviewing once (p=0.11)

claims.text: Cross-Context Review works with any model.
Cross-Context Review costs only one extra session.
The benefit of Cross-Context Review comes from context separation itself.
claims.source_quote: The benefit comes from context separation itself.
